claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have a child or children that you're responsible for? If yes, how old is each child?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have a child. I have Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. So you have Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Since Alex is 8 years old (under 16), that's good - children under 16 automatically qualify age-wise. 

Now I need to ask: Does Alex work less than 24 hours per week (or is not in paid work at all)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all. They're only 8, so they're just at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's perfect. Since Alex is 8 years old and not working, they meet that requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Next question: Is Alex receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, that's another requirement met. 

Next: Is Alex in an apprenticeship program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in an apprenticeship. They're only 8, so they're just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to ask about your circumstances.

Do you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect. You mentioned earlier that Alex lives with you at home, so that confirms you're responsible for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Next question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex (such as another parent or guardian)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good. Now I need to ask about Alex's living situation. Is Alex:
- In hospital?
- In care (looked after by the local council)?
- In a foster care arrangement?
- Or living normally at home with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is in a foster care arrangement. I'm fostering them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. I need to ask about the fostering arrangement to determine eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I need to ask: Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance as part of the fostering arrangement?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, the local authority gives me maintenance for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to make a determination about your Child Benefit eligibility.

Based on what you've told me, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**. 

Here's why: While Alex meets the age requirements (being 8 years old) and you are responsible for them, the key issue is that the local authority is paying maintenance towards Alex as part of the fostering arrangement. Under Child Benefit rules, you can only claim Child Benefit for a fostered child if the local council is NOT paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Since the council is providing you with maintenance payments for Alex, this makes you ineligible for Child Benefit in this situation. The maintenance payments from the local authority are intended to cover the costs of caring for Alex, which is why Child Benefit is not also available.

If the fostering arrangement changes in the future and the council stops paying maintenance, you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit at that point. You could check with the local authority about their payment arrangements if your circumstances change.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I see. That's disappointing, but I understand. Thank you for explaining that to me. No, I think that's all I needed to know.
